Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health
Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program (CPSP) Provider Information
The Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program (CPSP) is a free program that helps improve the health of pregnant women who are low-income and give their babies a healthy start. CPSP-certified doctors who follow the program's guidelines can get extra payments through Medi-Cal. The program aims to lower the number of babies born with low weight and improve pregnancy outcomes. It does this by including support for nutrition, mental health, health education, and pregnancy care along with regular medical care.
CPSP Provider Enrollment
Providers must apply and be certified by the California State Department of Health to take part in the program.
Enrollment Requirements:
Applicant's site must be actively enrolled in Medi-Cal under an organizational National Provider Identifier (NPI) and be a CPSP-eligible provider type.
Applicants must have an approved CPSP Provider Application on file with CDPH/MCAH.
Eligible CPSP Provider Types:
Physician specializing in obstetrics and gynecology, pediatrics, family medicine, or general medicine
Physician group
Certified nurse midwife (CNM)
Licensed midwife (LM)
Hospital outpatient clinic
Community clinic
County clinic
Alternative birth center
Want to Enroll?
